title: boronia crenulata subsp. crenulata
intro: Boronia crenulata subsp. crenulata is a subspecies of the Boronia crenulata plant, a flowering plant native to eastern Australia, characterized by its fragrant white or pale pink flowers and its use in perfumery. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of volatile oils and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Boronia crenulata subsp. crenulata are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. It is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used for aromatherapy or applied topically for their therapeutic effects.
